reasoning: The question asks if there will be more than 10 times as many 'Battles' in the Falkland Islands compared to a baseline metric. The background indicates that the reference value is exactly 1.000, meaning the 30-day average has been exactly 0. To trigger the resolution criteria, there would need to be 11 or more 'Battles' (10 x 1 = 10, thus 'more than 10' means >=11). A 'Battle' is defined as violent interactions between two organized armed groups. The Falkland Islands are extremely peaceful. There is no internal conflict and the UK defends them. An invasion by Argentina is the only conceivable scenario to generate battles. However, Javier Milei has consistently favored peaceful solutions for Argentina eventually obtaining the Falklands and the UK maintains a strong deterrent. The chance of a completely sudden war involving at least 11 distinctive armed clashes within any of the 30-day periods preceding the resolution dates is virtually zero. However, we're required to bound our probability above 0, and rules suggest (0.02-0.98), so the lowest output is 0.02 across all dates. The time horizons (Nov 2025, Dec 2025, Feb 2026) are very short and don't meaningfully change the risk.
